market_context: Whipsaw chip tape: Benzinga VOO/market wraps show a 'Chip Bloodbath' on 06-23, Micron rallying 15% while Apple sank 6% on memory costs on 06-25 (Benzinga 'Micron Rallies 15%, Apple Sinks 6% On Memory Costs'), a 06-26 'Chip Rout Drags Nasdaq 100', then a 06-29 'Nasdaq 100 Soars As Semis Rebound'. Macro is headline-driven and unstable, so the default today is hold across the watchlist. Portfolio is 100% cash (no holdings per data/2026-06-30/_manifest.json), so no current-holdings news sweep was required. DATA GAP: fundamentals (including sector) are unavailable for every ticker today — the yfinance source host fc.yahoo.com was blocked by the egress policy (403 CONNECT), recorded in each snapshot's errors list. Because the decision engine cannot enforce the sector cap without a sector, no buy could be validated today regardless; default hold is doubly warranted. Prices and 30-day bars (Alpaca IEX) and Alpaca news were fetched cleanly.

signals.thesis: Volatile, no clean trigger: close 281.69 (06-29) sits well below the early-June ~315 high after a 6% 06-25 drop on memory-cost concerns. Benzinga 06-30 'CMA Proposes New Apple And Google Mobile Platform Rules To Let UK App Developers Steer Users To Off-Platform Payments' is UK payments regulation, not an earnings/guidance catalyst. Hold; revisit on a concrete margin/guidance datapoint or a break below 270.
